125364.explanation: With equal suitability and availability, the municipal executive board can consist of an equal number of men and women.

125365.explanation: The municipality of Veenendaal must focus on extensive participation of residents in important matters; referendums can support this.

125368.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have room for customized opening hours, as long as this fits within agreements on livability, safety, and the interests of local residents.

125369.explanation: Housing asylum seekers is a national responsibility. Currently, we already have long-term shelter in Veenendaal. We pay attention to livability and safety in neighborhoods.

125375.explanation: At festivals and parties, a good balance must be found between liveliness and livability. Customized arrangements, clear agreements with residents, and enforcement are more important than structurally tightening sound standards.

125376.explanation: The municipality must promote and facilitate accessibility, for example with advice, customization, and targeted support where necessary and effective, without structural subsidies for all entrepreneurs.

125378.explanation: The municipality must ensure that all primary school children can come into contact with music and culture, and that children with talent or from low-income families receive extra support.

125379.explanation: Sports clubs are of great value to Veenendaal. If they encounter problems, the municipality must work with them to determine what support is needed and responsible, financially or otherwise.

125380.explanation: Visible and easily accessible enforcement officers in the neighborhood are essential for a safe Veenendaal. They enhance the feeling of safety and provide swift follow-up on reports.

125382.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must always be quickly and safely accommodated. The municipality must organize this well through regional facilities and partners, with sufficient capacity and accessibility.

125383.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to do their work safely. The municipality should assess per task and situation which means are necessary and proportionate, with good training and clear frameworks.

125387.explanation: Homes should primarily be intended for living in. The municipality can encourage or require owner-occupancy where it helps better protect starters and local residents, within clear and reasonable exceptions.

125388.explanation: The municipality must focus on sustainable and permanent housing construction for local residents. Temporary housing may only be used in exceptional situations and should not come at the expense of structural solutions.

125389.explanation: People on social assistance are expected to participate in society according to their abilities, for example through volunteering, education, or reintegration, considering their personal situation. It is also important for residents themselves to (be able to) participate, including for social contacts.

125390.explanation: The municipality must protect low-income residents from financial overload and prevent stress. Forgiveness of municipal taxes remains an important safety net.

125391.explanation: Veenendaal has a generous social assistance policy. The municipality must prevent people just above the income limit from falling between the cracks by providing customization and targeted support where needed.

125398.explanation: The municipality must focus on feasible and affordable sustainable mobility, with stimulation and customized approaches instead of prohibitive measures like environmental zones. Implementation can only be coordinated with the region, avoiding unnecessary frustration of entrepreneurs.

125400.explanation: When providing care, as much as possible should be taken into account regarding residents’ religious and cultural backgrounds, within the limits of availability and affordability.

125401.explanation: Quality and effectiveness must be leading in youth care. Too strong a focus on price often leads to poorer care and higher long-term costs.

125402.explanation: The municipality must ensure that low-income residents have access to necessary basic products, including menstrual products, for example through targeted support or existing schemes.

125403.explanation: The municipality must be cautious about levying property tax (ozb) and reduce or limit charges where possible, as long as this fits within a healthy financial management.

125404.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair tax. Good facilities, including for dog owners, are a common interest and should be funded from general resources.

125406.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide whether to open on Sundays themselves. Fewer shopping Sundays reduce freedom of choice and harm the liveliness of the center.

125409.explanation: Cultural facilities such as Theater Lampegiet are important for Veenendaal. The municipality must remain critical about efficiency and reach, but not stop supporting them.

125411.explanation: The municipality can support and encourage schools to pay attention to the Veens dialect and local history, without making it mandatory.

125412.explanation: The coffeeshop policy must focus on control and enforcement. Attention must be paid to a clean and safe living environment.

125414.explanation: Where appropriate to the surroundings, such as near the center or station areas, height can be used to add housing without compromising livability. This takes into account both the housing need and the importance of participation with local residents.

125417.explanation: The city center must remain easily accessible. The municipality should first better utilize existing parking spaces and only create additional parking solutions where necessary and appropriate.

125419.explanation: In good cooperation with neighboring municipalities, Veenendaal can grow smartly with attention to livability, mobility, greenery, and sufficient facilities. Growth contributes to building more homes for Veenendaal residents.

125422.explanation: Sundays are important for a lively city center. Additional parking costs form an unnecessary barrier for visitors and entrepreneurs. Moreover, we have understanding for attending church on Sundays.

125364.explanation: For the ChristenUnie, competencies, skills, knowledge, and experience are leading, and we value diversity in that respect.

125365.explanation: Municipal council members represent society. Referendums have proven to be difficult instruments. Therefore, ChristenUnie is cautious.

125368.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that peace is an important value for society. The municipality may regulate when the city is quiet.

125369.explanation: There is already an asylum seekers center on the site near Belcompany. ChristenUnie is glad with the facilities Veenendaal already provides for people in need.

125375.explanation: ChristenUnie has recently agreed to new stricter standards. Even stricter standards are not necessary for us at this time.

125376.explanation: ChristenUnie finds accessibility very important and at the same time a personal responsibility of entrepreneurs.

125378.explanation: ChristenUnie believes it is important that every child can make music, but the government does not need to pay for this for everyone. Children from low-income families can participate in sports or music through the Youth Fund for Sport & Culture.

125379.explanation: ChristenUnie believes there is already a lot of support for sports clubs. The municipality is responsible for the sports fields, but the clubs themselves are responsible for the (financial) management.

125380.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that supervision and enforcement strongly contribute to a safe and livable society.

125382.explanation: ChristenUnie finds it important that there are sufficient shelter options for people with unsafe home situations. Currently, this is well organized for the Veenendaal region.

125383.explanation: ChristenUnie acknowledges that enforcement officers (boa’s) are trained differently but increasingly face threatening situations. It is better to equip them or deploy more police. More police officers will not be available in coming years, making the baton an alternative.

125387.explanation: ChristenUnie believes a home is not an investment but an important basic provision for residents.

125388.explanation: ChristenUnie has no signals that there is a need for these homes in Veenendaal.

125389.explanation: ChristenUnie considers it a healthy principle that everyone contributes to society, always within the limits of reasonableness and fairness.

125390.explanation: ChristenUnie is pleased with current policies regarding exemptions for residents with low incomes.

125391.explanation: ChristenUnie has worked hard to maintain the current approach, in which mercy and justice have been well balanced in our opinion.

125398.explanation: ChristenUnie wants, in good consultation with entrepreneurs and residents in the center, to encourage restricting polluting vehicles but not to enforce it immediately.

125400.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that a good match between caregiver and patient greatly contributes to the effectiveness of treatment.

125401.explanation: ChristenUnie holds that careful selection based on qualification and competence is more important than the lowest treatment price, with price being part of the consideration.

125402.explanation: ChristenUnie always supports residents with low incomes, including regarding tampons and sanitary pads. However, this does not have to be provided free to everyone.

125403.explanation: ChristenUnie believes the municipality bears too many costs for matters that can demonstrably be financed by market parties, such as the theater. Therefore, the property tax (OZB) is unnecessarily high.

125404.explanation: ChristenUnie thinks that costs for maintaining dog areas and waste bins for droppings can be paid by dog owners.

125406.explanation: ChristenUnie wishes everyone a festive day of rest on Sundays.

125409.explanation: ChristenUnie opposes spending large amounts of money on a big new theater. Because culture has important social value, we find the current subsidy scheme fine.

125411.explanation: ChristenUnie does not consider it the schools' task to focus on the Veenendaal dialect.

125412.explanation: ChristenUnie believes coffeeshops and the substances they sell detract from personal and social well-being.

125414.explanation: ChristenUnie finds that current plans provide for sufficient high-rise buildings.

125417.explanation: ChristenUnie sees that there are currently enough parking spaces for cars. More parking space is needed for bicycles.

125419.explanation: ChristenUnie wants to explore possibilities for growth beyond existing municipal boundaries in good consultation with neighboring municipalities and provinces.

125422.explanation: For ChristenUnie, parking is free on Sundays just like on other holidays!

125364.explanation: Most important is that they are good administrators with a heart for Veenendaal.

125365.explanation: We have a representative democracy: residents may trust that their interests and the common good are well represented by those they have elected. There are sufficient other ways to properly involve residents in decisions.

125368.explanation: SGP is fundamentally against opening on Sundays. On other days, opening hours must be determined so that the livability for local residents and others is guaranteed.

125369.explanation: There is already shelter for asylum seekers and refugees in Veenendaal.

125375.explanation: Livability for the surroundings and our residents must be maintained and the nuisance must be minimal.

125376.explanation: The principle is that the entrepreneur pays the costs, but a financial boost might help the entrepreneur really make his building accessible.

125378.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of a child's parents or guardians. Through minimum income schemes, something can be offered for children of parents/guardians who cannot afford it.

125379.explanation: For the SGP, the principle is that the user pays. The municipality can, however, provide input on solutions and the use of any existing subsidy schemes.

125380.explanation: Enforcement is very important for the SGP. Offenders must not go unpunished, as that undermines our rule of law and the safety of our residents.

125382.explanation: There is already a facility where people from Veenendaal can also be accommodated.

125383.explanation: This is a police weapon. Weapons can also provoke violence.

125387.explanation: This cannot be enforced. However, we do find it important that no dire situations or exploitation of tenants arise.

125388.explanation: This is not primarily a task of the municipality. Attention must be paid to the target group of young people when developing housing.

125389.explanation: It is reasonable, if possible, to make a social contribution when someone receives benefits.

125390.explanation: The municipality must ensure that residents with a low income do not bear excessive burdens and can have a reasonable livelihood. That can be a reason to waive municipal taxes.

125391.explanation: Raising the income limit means that there may be people who need help but cannot receive it.

125398.explanation: We would like to see a as clean as possible city center; an environmental zone can help with that. It is important that distribution to the shops is not hindered; this can be done, for example, through a distribution hub. The SGP also welcomes investigating an exception for market traders who do not have an electric bus.

125400.explanation: Belief is important in people's lives, especially in situations of care and vulnerability. Therefore, it is very important that assistance can be aligned with this in social support.

125401.explanation: A reasonable price is important, but the quality of youth care is at least as important. The organization must also align with municipal youth care policy and not just focus on maximum financial turnover and profit.

125402.explanation: One option is to organize this, for example, through the Food Bank.

125403.explanation: The municipality should first look at reducing costs and only then at increasing taxes. The SGP prefers lowering taxes rather than spending a lot of money on a new theater.

125404.explanation: Having a dog leads to costs for the Veenendaal community (e.g., dog waste bags, dog parks, etc.). It is reasonable that the dog owner contributes to these costs.

125406.explanation: The SGP is fundamentally against Sunday shopping. A healthy society and a vital economy are served well by the other 6 days of the week. This is also evident from the failure of Sunday shopping in Veenendaal.

125409.explanation: The SGP has fundamental and financial objections to the theater. The new building leads to long-term high expenses for our residents, while few people from Veenendaal use it. There is hardly any positive effect on our economy, yet we are the cultural sponsor of the region.

125411.explanation: The SGP wholeheartedly encourages schools to convey Veenendaal's history and culture to children. The municipality can stimulate this even more, but it is not primarily the municipality's responsibility.

125412.explanation: The SGP is strongly against drug use because of the great harm to health and the high risk of addiction, which entails serious personal and societal negative consequences and damage. Therefore, definitely no establishment of a new coffee shop.

125414.explanation: Many people want to live in Veenendaal, for whom there are currently no homes. We will have to build high to provide housing for these people. The SGP wants the livability to be maintained or even improved: enough greenery, community spirit, play areas, etc.

125417.explanation: There must be a good balance between the need for and the supply of parking spaces.

125419.explanation: A better solution is to jointly develop new residential and business areas in good cooperation with neighboring municipalities.

125422.explanation: There is no financial necessity for paid parking on Sundays. Moreover, this means that many churchgoers must pay for parking on Sundays, which the SGP finds highly undesirable.

125364.explanation: It is about the quality of people; proportional division is not a goal in itself. It would be nice if we achieve an equal number of women and men.

125365.explanation: The CDA wants to involve residents in political choices and major decisions. Referenda reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice, which does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals.

125368.explanation: We want to be mindful of hospitality businesses with less regulatory pressure, balanced with livability for local residents.

125369.explanation: We offer a safe haven to people in need, while simultaneously taking into account the capacity and integration ability of Veenendaal's society.

125375.explanation: It is about balancing livability and bustle. Large-scale events go to the event terrain.

125376.explanation: Accessibility is a priority for us, in which everyone must take responsibility. Entrepreneurs are included in this.

125378.explanation: We support stimulating cultural activities through schools and MAAK Veenendaal.

125379.explanation: The municipality’s role is facilitating. We contribute practically but not financially.

125380.explanation: The CDA wants to deploy extra BOAs for safety and enforcement. So more visible blue uniforms on the streets.

125382.explanation: It has become clear that there is insufficient space in the Netherlands to accommodate these women. Veenendaal also takes responsibility here.

125383.explanation: BOAs need the proper equipment to be able to enforce effectively.

125387.explanation: We implement protection against speculators to prevent speculation by investment companies and to keep homes available for residents.

125388.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, we support flexible forms of housing, such as converting office buildings or using flexible homes.

125389.explanation: The CDA wants people to participate in society. A counterperformance must be meaningful and appropriate.

125390.explanation: The municipality must stop charging collection fees for people with minimum incomes.

125391.explanation: The municipality of Veenendaal already has a generous policy with attention for people who fall through the cracks.

125398.explanation: We want to keep regulatory pressure low. Enforcement is time-consuming and expensive.

125400.explanation: Care is often very personal, and it is important that people have freedom of choice in this.

125401.explanation: Quality and appropriate care are paramount. Not just the lowest price.

125402.explanation: There are many initiatives that provide for this (such as the distribution point for menstrual products from the poverty fund).

125403.explanation: CDA wants to keep the property tax (OZB) as low as possible, with a maximum inflation adjustment.

125404.explanation: Fees must be cost-covering. Not a disguised tax.

125406.explanation: We align ourselves with the existing policy.

125409.explanation: Veenendaal deserves a beautiful theater, and the municipality is investing in a new building. The Lampegiet is responsible for a balanced budget through cultural entrepreneurship.

125411.explanation: Veens is (unfortunately) not an existing regional language.

125412.explanation: There is sufficient supply in Veenendaal. Expansion is not necessary.

125414.explanation: We consider livability important in housing construction. High-rise buildings are only allowed in designated locations.

125417.explanation: There is still a lot of vacancy in the parking garages. However, sufficient parking spaces for the disabled must be ensured.

125419.explanation: We like to cooperate with neighboring municipalities to tackle the challenges in the housing market.

125422.explanation: We prefer to abolish paid parking altogether, including on Sundays.

125364.explanation: It is important that the council has a good balance between men and women because decision-making improves when the council truly reflects Veenendaal’s society.

125365.explanation: We as Local Veenendaal have supported a referendum for years on important matters close to everyone's heart. Residents in control!

125368.explanation: We at Local Veenendaal believe it is important that entrepreneurs can determine their own opening hours, as the entrepreneur knows exactly what their customers want.

125369.explanation: We think it is important that this question is first put to our residents via a referendum.

125375.explanation: The noise standards are already quite strict. Also, there are not many festivals in Veenendaal. Local Veenendaal finds the current regulations adequate.

125376.explanation: According to Local Veenendaal, everyone should be able to participate, and it is good that the government contributes financially to this.

125378.explanation: Yes, we as a municipality should promote music lessons.

125379.explanation: Sports clubs form the glue of society and deserve support from the municipality.

125380.explanation: We do not want BOAs (special enforcement officers) but more real police officers on the streets for our safety.

125382.explanation: Women or men who do not feel safe must be well protected. That is why we find it good that these safe houses are also established in Veenendaal.

125383.explanation: It is important that an enforcer can do their job well without feeling unsafe; a baton can then be very effective.

125387.explanation: Agreed, there is a housing shortage, so it is important that there is no room for exploitative landlords in the municipality of Veenendaal.

125388.explanation: There is a huge shortage of homes for young people who are currently forced to live at home too long. We want to solve this by building, building, and building.

125389.explanation: It is good for social contacts so you can find a real job faster, and meanwhile, it is valuable to use this time to contribute to society.

125390.explanation: It is important that everyone pays their share of taxes; income is already taken into account in tax calculations-the lower the income, the less tax is paid.

125391.explanation: The threshold in Veenendaal for receiving help with a low income is already much higher than the national average. Because the threshold is already high, we disagree with this statement.

125398.explanation: This would also mean many work vans of laborers, postal carriers, and trucks would no longer be welcome in our center to work, deliver mail, and supply goods to stores and horeca. This has major consequences for residents and entrepreneurs.

125400.explanation: Agreed, a care provider enters the personal life sphere of our residents, so it is important that the care provider understands the resident who needs care well.

125401.explanation: If possible, care should be purchased as cheaply as possible.

125402.explanation: For most people, this is affordable, and for residents who cannot afford it, the municipality should financially contribute.

125403.explanation: In recent years, the property tax (OZB) has risen sharply; we would like to partially reverse this.

125404.explanation: Yes, this is a tax that no longer fits the present time and has long been abolished in many other municipalities.

125406.explanation: No, entrepreneurs may decide for themselves whether to open their shops on Sundays.

125409.explanation: Agreed, the current subsidy is already more than sufficient. More money for the theater is not needed.

125411.explanation: It is very important that children learn at school how residents spoke Veens in the past; otherwise, this dialect will disappear entirely, which would be a shame.

125412.explanation: Soft drugs, like hard drugs, are bad for our youth, and we as a municipality should not promote them.

125414.explanation: Beautiful large residential flats that fit well in the living environment are a good solution to the housing scarcity; however, other residents must not suffer serious nuisance from this.

125417.explanation: There are still plenty of parking spaces for now, but it will only get busier, so the Municipality must start building new parking spaces in time.

125419.explanation: Veenendaal needs to grow; the housing shortage is high, so building is necessary, including on land from Rhenen and Ede.

125422.explanation: No, on Sundays many families also need to visit each other without having to pay extra costs, and on other days our residents already pay enough for parking in the center.

125364.explanation: A diverse team is important, but that does not mean the number of men and women need to be (exactly) equal. Ultimately, it is about the best person for the job. It is important, however, that men and women have equal opportunities.

125365.explanation: A referendum simplifies a often complex topic into a for/against proposition, leaving no room for nuance, substantiation, or alternative solutions. Of course, it is important that council members listen well to residents and stay in contact with the community.

125368.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve the freedom to set their own opening hours. Appropriate measures must be taken against (noise) nuisance. Moreover, longer opening hours result in a variation in closing times, which prevents a lot of nuisance.

125369.explanation: There is already an asylum seekers' center (AZC) with over 250 places, against which the VVD voted because safety for the surrounding area could not be sufficiently guaranteed. With these 250 places, Veenendaal has already made a substantial contribution. We oppose the establishment of a second AZC.

125375.explanation: The noise standards have been recently revised. The number of events with high noise levels is limited and leads to limited nuisance. There is therefore no reason to adjust the noise standards.

125376.explanation: Making a building accessible is the responsibility of the owner. If it is a municipal building, then the municipality must do that.

125378.explanation: Schools should provide a basic level of music education as part of their curriculum. It is up to the child and parents to decide whether to pursue further music lessons.

125379.explanation: Sports clubs are important, but financial support is not automatic. The municipality assesses each situation to determine if support is necessary and responsible. Self-reliance and club initiative are the starting points.

125380.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and the feeling of safety. More community enforcement officers (boa's) enable quicker action against nuisance and crime and support the police. This makes Veenendaal safer for everyone.

125382.explanation: A municipality the size of Veenendaal should have a shelter for women who would otherwise remain in unsafe situations.

125383.explanation: Equipping community enforcement officers with batons, pepper spray, and bodycams increases the safety of both the officers and residents. A prerequisite is that targeted training precedes this.

125387.explanation: The most important thing is that homes are inhabited and not unnecessarily vacant. Ownership is not essential. A healthy rental sector is also important for the necessary flexibility in the housing market.

125388.explanation: It is more effective to focus on housing market mobility than on temporarily housing students. The VVD prefers to use scarce space for real homes rather than containers.

125389.explanation: Having meaningful daytime activities positively affects everyone's well-being and is a stepping stone to paid work.

125390.explanation: Those who work (hard) should be rewarded by advancing more than those who do not. This is currently insufficient. We reform the local equalization system by eliminating above-statutory schemes, but there must be room for remission in exceptional cases.

125391.explanation: The municipality already has a generous minimum income policy with several arrangements available for incomes above 100% of the social assistance norm. It is unnecessary to further raise the income limits.

125398.explanation: Introducing an environmental zone in Veenendaal's center has almost no positive effects and causes many restrictions. We oppose unnecessary obstacles for entrepreneurs, residents, and visitors.

125400.explanation: Choices are important, but the freedom to choose care funded by the community is not unlimited.

125401.explanation: Quality and effectiveness must be leading. Of course, the municipality must weigh these against costs (price/quality), but costs should not be the only decisive factor.

125402.explanation: The municipality can support offering these products through, among other things, the food bank, and this already happens. The municipality does not have to do this itself.

125403.explanation: We want to keep municipal charges as low as possible. This can be done by focusing on what is truly important and by cutting unnecessary rules and projects. And during tight times, just like at home, we should be a bit more frugal and postpone non-urgent expenditures.

125404.explanation: There are facilities in Veenendaal specifically for dogs. The costs are covered by the dog tax. The dog tax will not be increased, but abolishing it would mean that all Veenendaal residents would have to pay for dog walking areas, including those without dogs.

125406.explanation: Shopkeepers may decide for themselves on which days to open their stores. Sunday shopping days do not cause nuisance or problems. For many people, Sunday is one of the few moments to do their shopping.

125409.explanation: The budget for construction is fixed and will not increase. For certain cultural performances, a contribution to the theater is necessary. These performances attract many visitors to Veenendaal and contribute to the vibrant city center from which all residents benefit.

125411.explanation: If primary schools want to initiate this themselves, that is fine, but it is not a priority or responsibility of the municipality.

125412.explanation: There are sufficient coffee shops. Additional coffee shops increase pressure on safety and livability.

125414.explanation: Houses must be built either vertically or horizontally. By allowing more high-rise buildings in a few places where urban planning allows it, we can prevent building in green areas. However, this must be with appropriate height and only where it is urban planning justified.

125417.explanation: Expansion is needed if there is a shortage of places, but it is not immediately necessary at this time.

125419.explanation: Building just outside the municipal boundaries is necessary. The land does not have to be owned by Veenendaal municipality, but building homes here in consultation with neighboring municipalities does offer growth space for Veenendaal.

125422.explanation: It is fairer to distribute the costs over all visitors, not just those coming Monday through Saturday. Then the hourly rates can be lowered.

125364.explanation: A good representation of society leads to better decisions. Equal opportunities and diversity in governance are very important. Unfortunately, this is not always fully controllable. It also depends on election results, available candidates, and coalition negotiations.

125365.explanation: A referendum simplifies a often complex issue into a yes/no question. Many complex topics are not suitable for this. We prefer more space for other forms of participation, allowing residents to engage more and better in policy-making.

125368.explanation: Entrepreneurs can already set their own opening hours. This should remain so. We would like to see our center become livelier. Agreements on livability remain important to ensure it remains pleasant for residents.

125369.explanation: We have a legal and moral duty to accommodate a number of asylum seekers in Veenendaal. There is already a reception center, and it must remain open.

125375.explanation: The noise standards are strict enough, in our opinion, and tailored to the environment. All events must take into account the surroundings and the health of visitors. But celebrations must also remain possible.

125376.explanation: Every building should be accessible to everyone. Primarily, we see this as the entrepreneur's responsibility. If for some reason this is not feasible, the municipality can consider financial or other support.

125378.explanation: Culture should not be a luxury. Every child deserves access to music lessons regardless of their parents' income. This helps development and self-confidence. We must, however, keep an eye on the costs.

125379.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role. It is important that as many people as possible can participate in sports. Therefore, sports clubs must be supported by the municipality.

125380.explanation: Safety is important, but we prefer to focus on prevention and youth work. If there is more nuisance at specific hotspots, additional enforcement officers (BOAs) can be deployed.

125382.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be able to be accommodated quickly and safely. We want sufficient shelter places, with regional cooperation being of great importance.

125383.explanation: BOAs must be able to work safely, but arming them is not the solution. We prefer good training and support. Good cooperation with the police is important, also to prevent BOAs from being assigned tasks that actually belong to the police.

125387.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in, not to make a profit. A self-occupancy obligation can help to reduce rising prices.

125388.explanation: It should be easier for young people to get housing. However, it is important that we pay close attention to the quality and facilities of temporary housing. At the same time, it should be possible to move on to affordable, permanent housing.

125389.explanation: Encouraging participation and work is good, but coercion often has the opposite effect. We prefer more guidance, education, and suitable work. Without punishment or humiliation. So that people can start suitable work more quickly.

125390.explanation: Debt remission prevents debts and stress. Residents who already have little should not be pushed further into hardship by municipal charges.

125391.explanation: Poverty also affects working people and middle incomes. Help must better align with reality so that more people can receive support. This prevents people from falling between the cracks.

125398.explanation: Clean air is important. An environmental zone helps to exclude the most polluting vehicles. With a proper transition period and exceptions where necessary.

125400.explanation: Good care fits the individual. It is important to have freedom of choice here so that care is appropriate. Quality and safety are paramount.

125401.explanation: Youth care must focus on quality. Too much focus on price leads to longer waiting times and possibly worse care. It is important that youth care remains affordable, therefore we support minimizing profits for providers.

125402.explanation: Menstrual products are basic necessities. No one should stay home due to lack of pads or tampons. By distributing them for free at schools and community centers, we combat period poverty and increase equality of opportunity.

125403.explanation: The municipality must be able to invest in facilities and policy. We want to distribute burdens fairly, with those who are strongest carrying the heaviest load.

125404.explanation: The proceeds from the dog tax can be used for dog facilities and keeping dog-walking areas clean and safe. However, we want this tax to be used only for these purposes and for facilities to be improved.

125406.explanation: Sundays mean different things to different people. Some want rest, others want to go out. We therefore want to give you space for both. That is why we maintain the shopping Sunday and allow more events on Sundays. So that you can choose how to spend the day.

125409.explanation: The new theater will be the cultural heart of Veenendaal. We want to provide space for amateur art, music, and local creators. It will be a place where everyone can experience something and be proud of.

125411.explanation: If schools want, the municipality can assist with this. Lessons about local identity and history can be valuable, but primary schools already have an overcrowded schedule.

125412.explanation: You should be able to buy safe weed and hash. This works well in Veenendaal now, and we want to keep it that way. There is currently room for coffee shops in Veenendaal. The municipality must have clear rules and be able to supervise. Young people receive clear information.

125414.explanation: Space in Veenendaal is limited and we need more housing. To ensure enough greenery and facilities, we will have to build higher. This way, housing can remain affordable. However, it must fit appropriately in the area.

125417.explanation: There are already many parking spaces in the center, and much vacancy in the parking garages. Extra spaces cost money and space, which the center does not have.

125419.explanation: Growth is not an end in itself. It is important that we build more homes, and we must do this in good cooperation with our neighboring municipalities. Not by simply taking over land.

125422.explanation: In places where you have to pay for parking, it makes sense to include Sundays as well. This way we can spread the cost over 7 days. To make this appropriate for all our residents, smart solutions (pre- or post-payment) can be included.

125364.explanation: D66 values both a good reflection of society and the quality of the council. With equal qualities, diversity is therefore preferred.

125365.explanation: For important issues, it is crucial that we listen well to residents. This can be done through a referendum, but also through good participation processes.

125369.explanation: D66 supports asylum reception with human dignity. As Veenendaal, we must take our fair share of the influx. By better European distribution and local responsibility, we can handle the influx and offer humane reception. However, there is already a reception center in Veenendaal.

125375.explanation: D66 supports a balance between enjoyment and possible nuisance. That balance must be determined per event and situation. Therefore, we oppose setting strict noise standards in advance.

125376.explanation: The municipality must help entrepreneurs make their businesses accessible. This, however, requires no financial contribution but mainly sharing knowledge and facilitating regulations.

125378.explanation: The municipality has a role in the cultural education of our children. Offering every child free music lessons, however, is not an effective measure. D66 would prefer the municipality to facilitate music lessons at school and support poor families with a financial contribution for music lessons.

125379.explanation: Veenendaal currently has a shortage of sports facilities for the current population. Since the population will only grow, it is important to at least maintain the existing offer. Therefore, the municipality must also play a financial role here.

125380.explanation: The municipality is growing, and this requires an increase in the number of BOAs. In D66's plans, we account for two additional BOAs by 2040.

125382.explanation: There is currently a national shortage of these housing types, causing many people to be housed in unsuitable locations. This is expensive and stressful. As the third city of the province, Veenendaal should also have this facility.

125383.explanation: D66 stands for a safe living environment. However, in the Netherlands only the police may use force. BOAs are a first line of control and serve partly as prevention. If things get out of hand, the police are necessary.

125387.explanation: In the current housing market crisis, where it is very hard to get a mortgage, it is important that there is sufficient mid-range rental housing available. For this reason, it is not necessarily bad if people rent out (part of) their home.

125388.explanation: Our municipality is full to the limits. Where building is possible, we should mainly build permanent housing. Also for young people and students.

125389.explanation: The municipality must deploy these people according to their strengths for meaningful work. This is good for Veenendaal’s society, where voluntary positions are always open. It is also good for the person on welfare, who remains engaged in society during the search for new work.

125390.explanation: Sometimes it is also cheaper for the municipality to forgive these taxes. It can also help these people through a difficult period, after which they can fully contribute again to municipal finances.

125391.explanation: D66 supports a municipality that looks ahead to tomorrow and cares for people. This also means providing financial support to those with less means. Currently, the threshold for this is 120% of the poverty line. For D66, this is a good limit.

125398.explanation: In the long term, this is a European requirement. Let us now announce an environmental zone for 2032. Then entrepreneurs know what to expect and can prepare. This prevents them from having to make last-minute additional investments.

125400.explanation: Everyone has free choice of care. For much of the care, the municipality is therefore not in charge, and people can choose for themselves. For care directly or indirectly offered by the municipality, this freedom of choice must also remain.

125401.explanation: The municipality must take a broad view of various aspects. A cheap organization with lower quality could also cause normally 'lighter' and cheaper youth care trajectories to escalate into 'heavier' and more expensive care, which could have been avoided otherwise.

125402.explanation: In places such as neighborhood centers, the library, and the town hall, the municipality should offer this free of charge.

125403.explanation: The housing costs in Veenendaal are below the national average. In principle, we should keep it that way, but further reduction is not necessary. Therefore, D66 is against it.

125404.explanation: D66 sees the dog tax as a user tax. A contribution from dog owners for the facilities the municipality provides. However, it should be carefully checked whether the revenues do not exceed the costs. If so, the tax should be reduced.

125406.explanation: For D66, every day of the week is equal. Therefore, a special arrangement on Sunday is not necessary. We must, however, take into account access to churches on this day. D66 also wants to increase the attractiveness of our shopping city on this day.

125409.explanation: Each visit to the theater brings entrepreneurs in our city approximately 2 euros. Losing the theater would therefore be very bad for our local economy. Investing in the theater is not only a cultural investment but also an investment in our entrepreneurs and our city center.

125411.explanation: That would really be a waste of education time.

125412.explanation: The municipality should pursue liberal establishment policies for new businesses, including coffee shops. However, this must be in an appropriate location. Not in the middle of a residential area or next to a school, for example.

125414.explanation: At appropriate locations, and in new neighborhoods at the borders of Veenendaal, we must certainly build upwards. Only then can we solve the housing crisis. D66 wants to build 20,000 new homes in and especially around Veenendaal. For example, near De Klomp and towards Rhenen.

125417.explanation: The center already has quite a few parking spaces. D66 wants to attract many more visitors to the center, but this can be done with the current number of parking spaces.

125419.explanation: D66 is in favor of a merger with Rhenen and Renswoude. Only then can we solve the housing market crisis. These municipalities do not have the capacity to realize much housing construction. Besides, these municipalities have already merged with Veenendaal on many points behind the scenes.

125422.explanation: For D66, all days of the week are equal. Therefore, we must apply the same rules on Sundays. However, we must think carefully about people who do not want to pay on Sundays for religious reasons. For example, by offering a Sunday subscription that can be paid on another day.

125364.explanation: A women's quota for high positions is a step forward, but it is only the beginning. The SP will continue to advocate for equal opportunities for women in all workplaces. Since 2018, we have had an all-male executive board; it is a myth that there are no qualified women.

125365.explanation: For this, the SP submitted an initiative proposal in 2023 to enable referendums in Veenendaal. All other parties voted against it. But that is no reason for us to give up because residents could actively make their voices heard through this means. We will keep striving for that.

125368.explanation: This cannot be unlimited. Especially in residential areas, the neighbors must also be protected. However, there is currently very little nightlife available in Veenendaal's center. The new pop venue that is coming thanks to the SP will give a nice boost to this.

125369.explanation: There is already a reception center (AZC) in Veenendaal. There is no need for a new one since we already meet all objectives. The wealthiest municipalities do the least; they should first find the next AZC locations now.

125375.explanation: There are legal standards for this, and we must adhere to them. In addition, the event grounds can be used much more often. To enable this, the site will be organized more professionally to attract entrepreneurs. In this way, liveliness and livability will go hand in hand.

125376.explanation: Well-accessible buildings are very important for people with disabilities. But they also increase the chance of survival in emergencies such as fires. However, the municipality cannot take this over for all entrepreneurs. There is legislation and national support. That is a more logical first route.

125378.explanation: Being able to express yourself through music is of great value. To ensure this is achievable for everyone, the SP advocates keeping it affordable for all. On the SP's proposal, it has succeeded in giving associations more income, which keeps membership fees lower. We will continue to focus on this.

125379.explanation: Sports are very important. To ensure that everyone can participate in sports, the Veenendaal SP has been advocating for years to keep it affordable for all. On the SP's proposal, we succeeded in giving clubs more income, which keeps membership fees lower. We will continue to focus on this.

125380.explanation: If you set rules, you also have to enforce them. At the same time, we must ensure that rules do not reward bad behavior. For example, requiring payment per supplied residual waste bin increases the chance of illegal dumping. So policy adjustments will certainly be necessary.

125382.explanation: The Veenendaal SP supports the effort for a safe home for everyone. Especially for people in need, we as a community and municipality must take an extra step. A women's shelter can be an important part of this.

125383.explanation: As the SP, we would prefer that this would not be necessary. However, we also see that guaranteeing the safety of enforcement officers (BOAs) is becoming increasingly difficult. Therefore, the possibility of providing a short baton is understandable. We must certainly stand up for the safety of our employees.

125387.explanation: With the current housing shortage, the municipality must introduce a general compulsory owner-occupancy rule. This means that you must live in a purchased home yourself. This gives our residents more chance of suitable housing. For this, we must also truly build affordable homes. That is our commitment.

125388.explanation: Even more important is building the right types of homes. Currently, we are building homes that are too expensive. Making truly affordable new homes available is a real solution. As a temporary step, this can provide relief, but not a definitive long-term solution.

125389.explanation: We wholeheartedly welcome volunteer work, but regular work must also be properly rewarded. Volunteer work will always be voluntary. As far as the Veenendaal SP is concerned, we guarantee people the right to work with an appropriate income. We do not allow work without pay.

125390.explanation: The Veenendaal SP wants to abolish various municipal taxes (including waste collection charges, sewage charges, and dog tax). This saves costs for organizing them and can be incorporated into the already low property tax (OZB). With this, we want 75% of residents to pay less.

125391.explanation: The group of working poor continues to grow, and we are making significant national efforts. But as long as the government does not come up with solutions, we will have to take local steps forward. That is why the SP is committed to supporting the growing group of residents who need assistance whenever possible.

125398.explanation: We support our Veenendaal entrepreneurs in the shopping center. To ensure that visitors keep coming, we must not create unnecessary obstacles. Where the municipality can help make private and public transport more environmentally friendly, we support this. But we do not want to hassle motorists.

125400.explanation: Care is not a market and should be seriously reviewed. As the SP, we guarantee that we will not cut funding for care. Care also means it should fit the person receiving it. The GP or medical specialist has an active role in this. We leave that effort where it belongs.

125401.explanation: Cheap often turns out to be expensive. Youth care should not involve market forces at all. However, it currently does. Experience shows that some parties promote themselves as cheap but ultimately deliver no quality. It’s about balancing costs and quality of services.

125402.explanation: Poverty is a big problem, also in Veenendaal. Many people are ashamed of it. Dividing this poverty into groups (energy poverty, etc.) does not work. But that does not change that there really is a problem. In logical locations, the municipality can provide support together with society.

125403.explanation: The Veenendaal SP wants to abolish various municipal taxes (including waste collection charges, sewage charges, and dog tax). This saves costs for organizing them and can be incorporated into the already low property tax (OZB). With this, we want 75% of residents to pay less.

125404.explanation: The Veenendaal SP abolishes various municipal taxes (including waste collection charges, sewage charges, and dog tax). This saves costs for organizing them and can be incorporated into the already low property tax (OZB). With this, we want 75% of residents to pay less.

125406.explanation: Entrepreneurs may decide for themselves whether to open or not. We see that entrepreneurs know how to use this freedom appropriately. We should continue to trust that.

125409.explanation: A livable society also requires facilities that contribute to this. It is very good that next to the theater, now thanks to the great efforts of the SP, the pop venue is returning. This is of great value to many residents. The small hall will also provide space for much local talent.

125411.explanation: This is not a task for local government. Attention to our history is certainly important, but this is not the way to approach it. Teachers already have a lot on their plates; let’s not burden them unnecessarily further.

125412.explanation: A legal trading location has multiple advantages. In safety, quality, and controllability. The municipality should therefore not be opposed to the arrival of a new coffee shop.

125414.explanation: Veenendaal is one of the most densely built municipalities. We must be realistic that within the current territory, the limits are increasingly in sight. Livability and maintaining all facilities must therefore receive special attention. We need more land, and we are honest about that...

125417.explanation: There is already a considerable surplus at this moment. More parking spaces would only lead to higher costs but would not bring more visitors.

125419.explanation: Veenendaal is one of the most densely built municipalities. We must be realistic that within the current territory, the limits are increasingly in sight. Livability and maintaining all facilities must therefore receive special attention. We need more land, and we are honest about that...

125422.explanation: At the end of 2023, there was a proposal to charge for parking on Sundays. Thanks in part to the efforts of the SP, this was stopped. We will continue to fight against the privatization or commercialization of public parking spaces.

statements.title: Veenendaal should take over even more land from neighbouring municipalities in order to be able to grow.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: Veenendaal should hire more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The residents of Veenendaal should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Veenendaal moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The BOAs (special enforcement officers) who monitor safety in Veenendaal should be given batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Veenendaal controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Veens (the local dialect).
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: More high residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Veenendaal.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Veenendaal should have a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Veenendaal should introduce a low-emission zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The municipality should stop funding Lampegiet Theatre.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality must allow fewer Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Veenendaal should also have paid parking on Sundays.
